chunks.text: ORDER SHEET HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI C.P. No.D-1101 of 2020 ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Date Order with signature of Judge ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- 1. For hearing of CMA No.5183/2020 (U/O 39) 2. For hearing of main case ------------------------------------------- 01.10.2020. Dr. Muhammad Tariq Masood, advocate for the petitioner. Mr. Kafeel Ahmed Abbasi, DAG for respondents No.1 to 4. Ms. Amna Salman Ahmed, advocate for respondent No.5 along with Asif Hanif, DGM-I, (Store). >><< The instant petition has been filed on the ground that the respondent No.4 is not issuing ‘Exemption Certificate’ to the petitioner from withholding tax which is causing serious prejudice to it, as in absence of ‘Exemption Certificate’ the respondent No.5 is deducting withholding tax on auctioned goods and the same are not being released by the respondent No.5. All the learned counsel have been heard and with their consent the instant petition is disposed of with the following directions: 1. Application moved to the respondent No.4 /Commissioner Inland Revenue, which is pending adjudication, would be decided by him within fifteen days from the date of receipt of this order. 2. That the respondent No.5 /Sui Southern Gas Company Limited would not present the Pay Orders furnished by the petitioner and in case those Pay Orders are about to expire, the respondent No.5 will inform the petitioner in this regard, who will get the same validated for another six months’ period and thereafter petitioner would be allowed to lift the auctioned goods as per the requisite procedure of respondent No.5(SSGCL).

Application moved to the respondent No.4 /Commissioner Inland Revenue, which is pending adjudication, would be decided by him within fifteen days from the date of receipt of this order. 2. That the respondent No.5 /Sui Southern Gas Company Limited would not present the Pay Orders furnished by the petitioner and in case those Pay Orders are about to expire, the respondent No.5 will inform the petitioner in this regard, who will get the same validated for another six months’ period and thereafter petitioner would be allowed to lift the auctioned goods as per the requisite procedure of respondent No.5(SSGCL). Petition stands disposed of in the above manner along with the listed application. JUDGE JUDG Gulzar/PA
